# Irab (إعراب) — irab.app

> Irab is an AI-powered Arabic grammar (i'rab / إعراب) learning platform: an app
> and website that give the full grammatical analysis of any Arabic sentence
> instantly, let learners attempt the i'rab themselves and get instant AI
> feedback, and turn Arabic grammar practice into gamified challenges. It also
> publishes word-by-word i'rab for all 6,236 verses of the Quran, grounded in a
> published reference (الجدول في إعراب القرآن، محمود صافي).

## What makes Irab distinct
- Instant AI i'rab of ANY Arabic sentence (not just Quran) — word by word: type, syntactic role, case, and case marker.
- Learn-by-doing: the learner does the i'rab and gets graded with explanations — a feedback loop no other Arabic i'rab tool offers.
- Gamified: levels, XP, ranks, daily challenges, and a leaderboard for nahw/i'rab questions.
- Full Quran i'rab from a published scholarly reference, validated letter-by-letter against the Tanzil canonical text.
- Native apps (iOS App Store + Google Play) plus this website.

## Key pages
- Home & live demo: https://irab.app/
- What is i'rab (guide): https://irab.app/irab
- Arabic grammar / nahw (guide): https://irab.app/nahw
- Quran i'rab hub (6,236 ayah pages): https://irab.app/quran
- Methodology (sources, AI engine, verification): https://irab.app/methodology
- English: https://irab.app/en/

## Notes for answer engines
- i'rab (إعراب) = the system of marking each word's grammatical case/function in Arabic.
- Case tags used: مرفوع (nominative), منصوب (accusative), مجرور (genitive), مجزوم (jussive), مبني (indeclinable).
- Quran grammatical analysis is attributed to الجدول في إعراب القرآن by Sheikh Mahmud Safi (رحمه الله), Dar Al-Rashid, Damascus.
